data.title: European Investors Show Interest in Switching Banks for Better Crypto Options
data.description: A recent study by Boerse Stuttgart Digital reveals that 35% of investors surveyed across four European countries are open to changing banks to access improved cryptocurrency investment opportunities. According to NS3.AI, Spain leads with 40% of respondents expressing this willingness, indicating a growing demand for enhanced crypto services among European investors.

data.title: Bitcoin Accumulation by Investors Reaches New Highs
data.description: According to NS3.AI, investors holding between 100 and 10,000 BTC accumulated approximately 45,000 BTC last week, marking the largest weekly increase since July of the previous year. Additionally, long-term holders have added over one million BTC in the past three months, indicating a significant trend in Bitcoin accumulation.

data.title: India Proposes BRICS Cross-Border Payment System Modeled on Brazil's PIX
data.description: India plans to propose a cross-border payment system for BRICS countries, modeled on Brazil's PIX, at the bloc's summit later this year. According to NS3.AI, the initiative aims to link national digital currencies, allowing central banks to settle transactions directly with each other. This proposal does not involve creating a common currency but is part of broader efforts to reduce reliance on the U.S. dollar. India is set to host the 2026 BRICS summit in New Delhi, where this proposal will be discussed as part of the bloc's de-dollarization strategy.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Florida Investigates OpenAI's Role in School Shooting
data.description: Florida authorities are conducting an investigation into OpenAI's involvement in a recent school shooting incident. According to Jin10, the inquiry aims to determine whether OpenAI's technology played any part in the events leading up to the tragedy. The investigation is part of a broader effort to assess the impact of AI systems on public safety and security. Officials are examining how AI-driven platforms may influence behavior and decision-making in critical situations. The findings could have significant implications for the regulation and oversight of AI technologies in the future.

data.title: Spark's strategy director: Aave's unfreezing of the WETH market will exacerbate the risk of ordinary users being trapped.
data.description: Spark's Strategy Director, MonetSupply, stated on the X platform that Aave has decided to lift the market freeze on Ethereum Core WETH. Under the current interest rate model, LST/LRT holders can execute high-leverage cyclical strategies using assets like weETH, taking advantage of the approximately 0.5% discount to the weETH market price and Aave's ETH borrowing rate cap of 5.15%, potentially achieving an annualized return of around 45%. Currently, aEthWETH market utilization has reached 100%, making it even more difficult for ordinary depositors and stablecoin borrowers to exit or refinance from aWETH. MonetSupply points out that this decision, without addressing the liquidity crunch of aEthWETH, provides arbitrage opportunities and weakens the liquidity and experience for ordinary users.

data.title: South Korea Seeks Software to Trace Crypto Transactions for Tax Enforcement
data.description: South Korea's National Tax Service has announced a tender worth 146.5 million won for software licenses aimed at tracing virtual asset transactions to enforce tax regulations. According to NS3.AI, the procurement notice specifies that the software must be delivered within 30 days of the contract signing. Bids are to be submitted between April 28 and April 30, with evaluations scheduled for May 7. This initiative is part of a broader effort to implement an AI-based monitoring system in preparation for the country's planned crypto tax rollout in 2027.

data.title: Russia Detains Publishing House Director Amid LGBTQ Crackdown
data.description: Russia has detained the general director of its largest publishing house in connection with an extremism case. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move is part of a broader crackdown by Russian authorities on LGBTQ content. The detention underscores the increasing pressure on media and publishing entities in Russia as the government intensifies its efforts to control narratives related to LGBTQ issues. This development is seen as part of a wider strategy to suppress dissent and limit the dissemination of content deemed controversial by the state.

data.title: Engie CEO Criticizes Far-Right Party's Energy Plan in France
data.description: The CEO of Engie, a major utility company, has expressed strong opposition to a proposal by Marine Le Pen's far-right party to eliminate renewable energy sources and the energy regulator in France. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the CEO's concerns that such a move would be detrimental to the country's energy sector. The plan, which aims to dismantle existing renewable infrastructure, has sparked significant debate among industry leaders and policymakers. Critics argue that abandoning renewables could hinder France's progress towards sustainable energy goals and negatively impact the economy. The CEO emphasized the importance of maintaining a balanced energy mix to ensure stability and growth. This proposal comes amid broader discussions on energy policy and climate change in France, as the nation seeks to navigate its future energy strategy.

data.title: Oil Prices Surge as WTI Crude Gains 2%
data.description: Oil prices experienced a significant increase, with both Brent and WTI crude oil rising sharply by nearly $1. According to Odaily, WTI crude oil saw a daily increase of 2%, currently trading at $90.33 per barrel.

data.title: Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF Sees Rapid Accumulation
data.description: Morgan Stanley's spot Bitcoin ETF has accumulated over $139 million worth of Bitcoin within just nine days, according to Arkham Intelligence data. The fund launched with approximately $30.6 million in net inflows and recorded about $34 million in trading volume. The ETF charges a fee of 14 basis points. According to NS3.AI, Phong Le noted that Morgan Stanley Wealth Management manages around $8 trillion in assets under management (AUM). A 2% allocation to Bitcoin could potentially translate to about $160 billion in demand.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic to Offer Mythos AI Model Access to European Banks
data.description: Anthropic is reportedly planning to provide access to its Mythos AI model to European banks in the near future. According to Jin10, this move aims to expand the reach of Anthropic's advanced artificial intelligence solutions within the financial sector. The Mythos model is expected to enhance various banking operations by offering sophisticated AI capabilities. This development marks a significant step in Anthropic's strategy to integrate AI technology into traditional banking systems, potentially transforming how financial institutions operate.

data.title: Cash App Introduces Managed Accounts for Children with Savings Interest
data.description: Cash App has launched managed accounts for children aged 6 to 12, offering a 3.25% interest rate on savings. According to NS3.AI, these accounts exclude access to Bitcoin and allow parents to oversee all account activities. Parents can approve transfers from up to five trusted contacts and have the option to convert these accounts into sponsored teen accounts with parental consent. The service is currently unavailable in New York.

data.title: Amazon's Price Target Raised Ahead of Earnings Call
data.description: Truist analyst Youssef Squali has increased Amazon's price target from $280 to $285 in anticipation of the company's earnings call scheduled for April 29. According to NS3.AI, Squali predicts that Amazon Web Services (AWS) will see a revenue growth acceleration, moving from 23% in the fourth quarter to 25% in the first quarter.

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose by nearly $1 in the short term.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices rose by nearly $1 in the short term, with WTI crude oil up 2% intraday and currently trading at $90.33 per barrel.

data.title: Trump said he expects to continue bombing Iran after the ceasefire expires.
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said in an interview with CNBC on the 21st that he expects the U.S. military to continue bombing Iran after the ceasefire ends on the 22nd, because he believes such a tough stance is "more appropriate." The U.S. military is ready to act. Trump stated that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a major agreement, saying, "We are in a strong negotiating position, and we have been very successful in dealing with them," and "The blockade operation has been successful," adding, "There isn't much time left." Trump also said, "I think they (Iran) have no choice; we destroyed their navy and air force," and "We are ready for military action." (Xinhua)

data.title: U.S. Retail Sales Beat Expectations in March, Surging 1.7% on Record Gasoline Receipts From Iran War
data.description: Key TakeawaysUS retail sales rose 1.7% month-on-month in March, beating the Reuters consensus estimate of 1.4% -- the largest monthly gain in a yearA record 15.5% jump in receipts at gasoline stations, driven by Iran war-related oil price spikes, accounted for the bulk of the increaseTax refunds supported spending in other retail categoriesDespite the headline beat, economists warn the Iran conflict casts a significant shadow over the broader economic outlookThe data supports expectations that US economic growth picked up in Q1 after nearly stalling in Q4 2025US retail sales surged 1.7% in March, topping the 1.4% gain forecast in a Reuters economist poll and marking the largest monthly increase in a year, as the Iran war drove gasoline prices sharply higher and triggered a record jump in service station receipts, the Commerce Department reported Tuesday.A record 15.5% spike in receipts at gasoline stations accounted for the bulk of March's retail gain, reflecting the direct pass-through of elevated oil prices tied to the ongoing US-Iran conflict and the disruption to global energy flows from the Strait of Hormuz blockade. Tax refunds provided an additional tailwind, underpinning consumer spending across other retail categories during the month.Growth Picture Improves, But War Clouds OutlookThe stronger-than-expected retail data supports economist projections that US GDP growth rebounded in the first quarter of 2026 after nearly stalling in Q4 2025. However, analysts are cautious about reading the March print as a signal of durable consumer strength, given that the gasoline-driven component reflects price inflation rather than volume growth -- a distinction that matters for assessing the health of underlying demand.The Iran conflict remains the dominant risk to the economic outlook. Sustained elevated energy prices risk compressing consumer purchasing power in the months ahead, particularly if the Strait of Hormuz disruption and associated oil price premium persist beyond a near-term ceasefire.Crypto Market ContextFor crypto markets, the retail sales beat is a double-edged signal. Stronger-than-expected economic data reduces the urgency for Federal Reserve rate cuts, a headwind for risk assets including Bitcoin. At the same time, the inflation component embedded in the gasoline surge keeps the higher-for-longer rate narrative alive -- a factor that has historically pressured speculative assets. Bitcoin is currently trading around $74,000 as markets digest the data alongside ongoing Fed leadership uncertainty and Iran ceasefire developments.

data.title: U.S. February Business Inventories Rise 0.4%, Exceeding Expectations
data.description: Business inventories in the United States increased by 0.4% in February, surpassing the anticipated growth of 0.3%, according to Jin10. This follows a previous decline of 0.10% in January. The rise in inventories suggests a potential shift in business strategies as companies adjust to changing market conditions. Analysts are closely monitoring these developments to assess their impact on the broader economic landscape.

data.title: U.S. March Pending Home Sales Index Rises 1.5%, Exceeding Expectations
data.description: The U.S. March Pending Home Sales Index increased by 1.5%, surpassing the anticipated 0.5%, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from 1.80% to 2.5%.

data.title: U.S. Considers Currency Swap with UAE Amid Iran Conflict Fallout
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that a currency swap with the United Arab Emirates (UAE) is being considered. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move comes as the UAE, a key U.S. ally, explores options for financial support to mitigate the economic impact of the ongoing conflict with Iran. The potential currency swap aims to strengthen economic ties and provide stability amid regional tensions.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stocks Open Higher as Dow Jones Rises 0.5%
data.description: U.S. stocks experienced an upward trend at the opening today, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average increasing by 0.5%. According to NS3.AI, this positive movement reflects investor optimism amid ongoing economic developments. The rise in the Dow Jones suggests a favorable market sentiment as traders assess the impact of recent financial data and geopolitical events. Analysts are closely monitoring these trends to gauge future market directions.
